Name: 2026 Toro GrandStand 48-inch Zero-Turn Mower (72504)
Short description: Toro GrandStand 48-inch Zero-Turn Mower (72504) — The 48-inch deck handles the lawn in half the time for the half-acre-to-one-acre customer. Authorised Toro dealer service at Reyco.
Description:
If you've got a property where straight rows and tight pivots both matter, the Toro GrandStand 48-inch Zero-Turn Mower (72504) is built for it. Zero-turns trade tractor steering for raw productivity — once you're comfortable on one, you'll cut the same lawn in half the time, with cleaner trim around obstacles.

Out across the lakefront properties, where most cottage and rural lots are obstacle courses (trees, septic mounds, gardens, gravel pads), the zero-turn pivot is what saves the time. The Toro GrandStand 48-inch Zero-Turn Mower (72504) sits in the sweet spot for a half-acre to a full acre — anything smaller and the deck size is overkill; anything bigger and you'd want a commercial unit. The 48-inch deck pulls cleanly through long grass without bogging, which matters when the rain runs the grass three days past mowing day. Aaron points the customers who'd grown up on tractors toward this one when the lot makes the case for it — and most aren't sorry about the switch.

Reyco is an authorised Toro dealer — warranty work and platform-specific service come through our shop. Aaron runs the service side as Service Manager; Lynn manages the parts counter so wear items are usually a same-day pickup. That matters more on a zero-turn than on most platforms — the hydrostatic drive units, the spindle bearings, the deck mounts are all wear items that benefit from being looked at by a tech who knows the line. We see Toro zero-turns come back on schedule for routine deck-belt and spindle service around year four, which is normal. Less drama, more uptime.

For the retiree who finally bought the equipment he always wanted, the Toro GrandStand 48-inch Zero-Turn Mower (72504) is the upgrade that changes the relationship to the lawn — Saturdays back, cleaner cut, less time on the seat. Call 705-253-7828 or stop in at 11 White Oak Drive East — Aaron or Damian will walk you through what fits your property.

Name: 2026 Cub Cadet XT2 LX42 42-inch Riding Mower
Short description: Cub Cadet XT2 LX42 42-inch Riding Mower — The 42-inch deck handles a real Northern Ontario lawn. Authorised Cub Cadet dealer service from Aaron and Damian at White Oak Drive.
Description:
Every spring at Reyco we move a stack of Cub Cadet XT2 LX42 42-inch Riding Mowers off the floor — usually to the cottage owner with a one-acre lakefront lot. Cub Cadet's XT2 platform is the one Aaron recommends when somebody walks in describing a real lawn (not just a city lot) and asks for a unit that'll start every Saturday for the next ten years.

The XT2 platform is Cub Cadet's ride-on done right: hydrostatic drive (push the pedal, it goes), a comfortable seat for the long pass, and an electric PTO so engaging the deck is one button instead of a pull. For the customer mowing a half-acre Sault property up through a full lakefront acre, the geometry works — three or four passes per side and you're done. Up here, where the mowing window runs from late May to early October, you want a unit you can trust to start every Saturday — and that's where Cub Cadet's residential line earns its money. Aaron's been managing the service side here long enough to tell you which tier of the lineup actually pays off and which one's just hood badges — drop in and he'll walk you through it.

Reyco is an authorised Cub Cadet dealer — warranty work happens here in Sault Ste. Marie, no shipping the unit elsewhere. Aaron oversees the service side as Co-Owner and Service Manager; Damian handles the lawn-and-snow bench end to end; Lynn manages the parts counter. For the retiree who finally bought the equipment he always wanted, the trade-off pencils out: a unit that'll hold up across a decade of weekend mowing on the mixed cottage-and-rural lawns we get up here, with parts and service running through one shop instead of three. That's the part of the buy-here pitch that matters past year three — when the spindle starts to clunk or the deck belt walks, it's a half-hour conversation with Damian, not a six-week parts order.

Cub Cadet has been part of Reyco's lawn line since the early days, and we've serviced enough of these to know what holds up. Call 705-253-7828 to talk it through, or visit the showroom at 11 White Oak Drive East. We'll make sure the unit fits the lot before you leave the lot.

Name: 2025 Toro TimeCutter 50-inch Zero-Turn Mower (77502)
Short description: Toro TimeCutter 50-inch Zero-Turn Mower (77502) — The 50-inch deck handles the lawn in half the time for the half-acre-to-one-acre customer. Authorised Toro dealer service at Reyco.
Description:
For the property where you've been adding fifteen minutes to the mow every season as the trees grow in, the Toro TimeCutter 50-inch Zero-Turn Mower (77502) is the answer. Toro's TimeCutter platform makes the obstacle course around the firepit, the playset, and the gardens an actual zero-turn job instead of a tractor-and-back-up dance.

Out across Northern Ontario, where most cottage and rural lots are obstacle courses (trees, septic mounds, gardens, gravel pads), the zero-turn pivot is what saves the time. The Toro TimeCutter 50-inch Zero-Turn Mower (77502) sits in the sweet spot for a half-acre to a full acre — anything smaller and the deck size is overkill; anything bigger and you'd want a commercial unit. The 50-inch deck pulls cleanly through long grass without bogging, which matters when the rain runs the grass three days past mowing day. Aaron points the customers who'd grown up on tractors toward this one when the lot makes the case for it — and most aren't sorry about the switch.

Reyco is an authorised Toro dealer — warranty work and platform-specific service come through our shop. Aaron runs the service side as Service Manager; Lynn manages the parts counter so wear items are usually a same-day pickup. That matters more on a zero-turn than on most platforms — the hydrostatic drive units, the spindle bearings, the deck mounts are all wear items that benefit from being looked at by a tech who knows the line. We see Toro zero-turns come back on schedule for routine deck-belt and spindle service around year four, which is normal. Less drama, more uptime.

For the in-town buyer with a flat lot and a tight Saturday schedule, the Toro TimeCutter 50-inch Zero-Turn Mower (77502) is the upgrade that changes the relationship to the lawn — Saturdays back, cleaner cut, less time on the seat. Call 705-253-7828 to talk it through, or visit the showroom at 11 White Oak Drive East. We'll make sure the unit fits the lot before you leave the lot.

Name: 2026 Cub Cadet XT1 LT46 46-inch Riding Mower IntelliPOWER
Short description: Cub Cadet XT1 LT46 46-inch Riding Mower IntelliPOWER — The 46-inch deck handles a real Northern Ontario lawn. Authorised Cub Cadet dealer service from Aaron and Damian at White Oak Drive.
Description:
Every spring at Reyco we move a stack of Cub Cadet XT1 LT46 46-inch Riding Mower IntelliPOWERs off the floor — usually to the property caretaker juggling four or five lakefront places. Cub Cadet's XT1 platform is the one Aaron recommends when somebody walks in describing a real lawn (not just a city lot) and asks for a unit that'll start every Saturday for the next ten years.

The XT1 platform is Cub Cadet's ride-on done right: hydrostatic drive (push the pedal, it goes), a comfortable seat for the long pass, and an electric PTO so engaging the deck is one button instead of a pull. Name: 2026 Toro TimeCutter 54-inch Zero-Turn Mower (77503)
Short description: Toro TimeCutter 54-inch Zero-Turn Mower (77503) — The 54-inch deck handles the lawn in half the time for the half-acre-to-one-acre customer. Authorised Toro dealer service at Reyco.
Description:
If you've got a property where straight rows and tight pivots both matter, the Toro TimeCutter 54-inch Zero-Turn Mower (77503) is built for it. Zero-turns trade tractor steering for raw productivity — once you're comfortable on one, you'll cut the same lawn in half the time, with cleaner trim around obstacles.

Out across the cottage roads east of town, where most cottage and rural lots are obstacle courses (trees, septic mounds, gardens, gravel pads), the zero-turn pivot is what saves the time. The Toro TimeCutter 54-inch Zero-Turn Mower (77503) sits in the sweet spot for a half-acre to a full acre — anything smaller and the deck size is overkill; anything bigger and you'd want a commercial unit. The 54-inch deck pulls cleanly through long grass without bogging, which matters when the rain runs the grass three days past mowing day. Aaron points the customers who'd grown up on tractors toward this one when the lot makes the case for it — and most aren't sorry about the switch.

Reyco is an authorised Toro dealer — warranty work and platform-specific service come through our shop. Aaron runs the service side as Service Manager; Lynn manages the parts counter so wear items are usually a same-day pickup. That matters more on a zero-turn than on most platforms — the hydrostatic drive units, the spindle bearings, the deck mounts are all wear items that benefit from being looked at by a tech who knows the line. We see Toro zero-turns come back on schedule for routine deck-belt and spindle service around year four, which is normal. Less drama, more uptime.

For the in-town buyer with a flat lot and a tight Saturday schedule, the Toro TimeCutter 54-inch Zero-Turn Mower (77503) is the upgrade that changes the relationship to the lawn — Saturdays back, cleaner cut, less time on the seat. If you've got questions about fit, call 705-253-7828. If you want to see the unit, we're at 11 White Oak Drive East in Sault Ste. Marie.

Name: 2026 Cub Cadet NX 15 SLX50 50-inch Riding Mower (13B5A2TLA10)
Short description: Cub Cadet NX 15 SLX50 50-inch Riding Mower (13B5A2TLA10) — The 50-inch deck handles a real Northern Ontario lawn. Authorised Cub Cadet dealer service from Aaron and Damian at White Oak Drive.
Description:
For the seasonal landscaper running multiple properties, the Cub Cadet NX 15 SLX50 50-inch Riding Mower (13B5A2TLA10) hits the sweet spot. Cub Cadet's NX platform is built around the customer who wants to finish the lawn before the mosquitoes come up — and the 50-inch deck does exactly that. We see this size move off the floor every spring at Reyco. It's the unit Aaron points cottage country buyers toward when they describe their property and ask 'what fits?'

The NX platform is Cub Cadet's ride-on done right: hydrostatic drive (push the pedal, it goes), a comfortable seat for the long pass, and an electric PTO so engaging the deck is one button instead of a pull. For the customer mowing a half-acre Sault property up through a full lakefront acre, the geometry works — three or four passes per side and you're done. Up here, where the mowing window runs from late May to early October, you want a unit you can trust to start every Saturday — and that's where Cub Cadet's residential line earns its money. Aaron's been managing the service side here long enough to tell you which tier of the lineup actually pays off and which one's just hood badges — drop in and he'll walk you through it.

Reyco is an authorised Cub Cadet dealer — warranty work happens here in Sault Ste. Marie, no shipping the unit elsewhere. Aaron oversees the service side as Co-Owner and Service Manager; Damian handles the lawn-and-snow bench end to end; Lynn manages the parts counter. For the homeowner with a tight half-acre suburban lot, the trade-off pencils out: a unit that'll hold up across a decade of weekend mowing on the Sault, with parts and service running through one shop instead of three. That's the part of the buy-here pitch that matters past year three — when the spindle starts to clunk or the deck belt walks, it's a half-hour conversation with Damian, not a six-week parts order.

Cub Cadet has been part of Reyco's lawn line since the early days, and we've serviced enough of these to know what holds up. Drop in at 11 White Oak Drive East to see it on the floor. Lynn manages the parts counter and Ron can pull most wear items same-day.

Name: 2025 Toro 77507 Zero-Turn Mower
Short description: Toro 77507 Zero-Turn Mower — Sized for the lawn in half the time for the half-acre-to-one-acre customer. Authorised Toro dealer service at Reyco.
Description:
If you've got a property where straight rows and tight pivots both matter, the Toro 77507 Zero-Turn Mower is built for it. Zero-turns trade tractor steering for raw productivity — once you're comfortable on one, you'll cut the same lawn in half the time, with cleaner trim around obstacles.

Out across the Sault, where most cottage and rural lots are obstacle courses (trees, septic mounds, gardens, gravel pads), the zero-turn pivot is what saves the time. The Toro 77507 Zero-Turn Mower sits in the sweet spot for a half-acre to a full acre — anything smaller and the deck size is overkill; anything bigger and you'd want a commercial unit. The deck pulls cleanly through long grass without bogging, which matters when the rain runs the grass three days past mowing day. Aaron points the customers who'd grown up on tractors toward this one when the lot makes the case for it — and most aren't sorry about the switch.

Reyco is an authorised Toro dealer — warranty work and platform-specific service come through our shop. Aaron runs the service side as Service Manager; Lynn manages the parts counter so wear items are usually a same-day pickup. That matters more on a zero-turn than on most platforms — the hydrostatic drive units, the spindle bearings, the deck mounts are all wear items that benefit from being looked at by a tech who knows the line. We see Toro zero-turns come back on schedule for routine deck-belt and spindle service around year four, which is normal. Less drama, more uptime.

For the seasonal landscaper running multiple properties, the Toro 77507 Zero-Turn Mower is the upgrade that changes the relationship to the lawn — Saturdays back, cleaner cut, less time on the seat. Call 705-253-7828 or stop in at 11 White Oak Drive East — Aaron or Damian will walk you through what fits your property.

Name: 2026 Cub Cadet XT1 LT46 46-inch Riding Mower (13APA9TTA10)
Short description: Cub Cadet XT1 LT46 46-inch Riding Mower (13APA9TTA10) — The 46-inch deck handles a real Northern Ontario lawn. Authorised Cub Cadet dealer service from Aaron and Damian at White Oak Drive.
Description:
For the seasonal landscaper running multiple properties, the Cub Cadet XT1 LT46 46-inch Riding Mower (13APA9TTA10) hits the sweet spot. Cub Cadet's XT1 platform is built around the customer who wants to finish the lawn before the mosquitoes come up — and the 46-inch deck does exactly that. We see this size move off the floor every spring at Reyco. It's the unit Aaron points cottage country buyers toward when they describe their property and ask 'what fits?'

The XT1 platform is Cub Cadet's ride-on done right: hydrostatic drive (push the pedal, it goes), a comfortable seat for the long pass, and an electric PTO so engaging the deck is one button instead of a pull. For the customer mowing a half-acre Sault property up through a full lakefront acre, the geometry works — three or four passes per side and you're done. Up here, where the mowing window runs from late May to early October, you want a unit you can trust to start every Saturday — and that's where Cub Cadet's residential line earns its money. Aaron's been managing the service side here long enough to tell you which tier of the lineup actually pays off and which one's just hood badges — drop in and he'll walk you through it.

Reyco is an authorised Cub Cadet dealer — warranty work happens here in Sault Ste. Marie, no shipping the unit elsewhere. Aaron oversees the service side as Co-Owner and Service Manager; Damian handles the lawn-and-snow bench end to end; Lynn manages the parts counter. For the customer who treats the lawn like a project, not a chore, the trade-off pencils out: a unit that'll hold up across a decade of weekend mowing on the mixed cottage-and-rural lawns we get up here, with parts and service running through one shop instead of three. That's the part of the buy-here pitch that matters past year three — when the spindle starts to clunk or the deck belt walks, it's a half-hour conversation with Damian, not a six-week parts order.

Cub Cadet has been part of Reyco's lawn line since the early days, and we've serviced enough of these to know what holds up. Stop by the showroom at 11 White Oak Drive East to see it on the floor, or call 705-253-7828 if you'd rather talk through whether it's the right fit.

Name: 2026 Cub Cadet XT1 ST54 54-inch Riding Mower FAB
Short description: Cub Cadet XT1 ST54 54-inch Riding Mower FAB — The 54-inch deck handles a real Northern Ontario lawn. Authorised Cub Cadet dealer service from Aaron and Damian at White Oak Drive.
Description:
If your lawn is enough work that a 42-inch deck would have you doing eight passes instead of five, the Cub Cadet XT1 ST54 54-inch Riding Mower FAB is the unit. Cub Cadet's XT1 platform was built around the property caretaker juggling four or five lakefront places — the customer with real grass to cut, not just a strip in front of the house.

The XT1 platform is Cub Cadet's ride-on done right: hydrostatic drive (push the pedal, it goes), a comfortable seat for the long pass, and an electric PTO so engaging the deck is one button instead of a pull. For the customer mowing a half-acre Sault property up through a full lakefront acre, the geometry works — three or four passes per side and you're done. Up here, where the mowing window runs from late May to early October, you want a unit you can trust to start every Saturday — and that's where Cub Cadet's residential line earns its money. Aaron's been managing the service side here long enough to tell you which tier of the lineup actually pays off and which one's just hood badges — drop in and he'll walk you through it.

Reyco is an authorised Cub Cadet dealer — warranty work happens here in Sault Ste. Marie, no shipping the unit elsewhere. Aaron oversees the service side as Co-Owner and Service Manager; Damian handles the lawn-and-snow bench end to end; Lynn manages the parts counter. For the customer who treats the lawn like a project, not a chore, the trade-off pencils out: a unit that'll hold up across a decade of weekend mowing on Northern Ontario, with parts and service running through one shop instead of three. That's the part of the buy-here pitch that matters past year three — when the spindle starts to clunk or the deck belt walks, it's a half-hour conversation with Damian, not a six-week parts order.

Cub Cadet has been part of Reyco's lawn line since the early days, and we've serviced enough of these to know what holds up. Stop by the showroom at 11 White Oak Drive East to see it on the floor, or call 705-253-7828 if you'd rather talk through whether it's the right fit.

Name: 2026 Toro TurfMaster 30-inch Push Mower (22217)
Short description: Toro TurfMaster 30-inch Push Mower (22217) — practical push mower for the smaller lot. Authorised Toro dealer at White Oak Drive.
Description:
For the smaller lot — the front yard, the boulevard strip, the rental — the Toro TurfMaster 30-inch Push Mower (22217) is the one we recommend. Not every lawn calls for a tractor. the cottager prepping a place for renters every weekend is often better off with a push mower that lives in the shed eleven months a year and starts cleanly in May.

A push mower lives or dies on three things: cold-start reliability, handle ergonomics, and how easy it is to service. Toro's residential push line is built around all three. The Toro TurfMaster 30-inch Push Mower (22217) is the one Aaron sells most across Algoma District — to retirees, to renters, to the in-town buyer with a tight lot. It's a unit that doesn't get talked about much because it works — and that's exactly the recommendation you want from a parts-counter shop, not a sales-floor pitch.

Reyco is an authorised Toro dealer — warranty work and platform-specific service come through our shop. Aaron runs the service side as Service Manager; Lynn manages the parts counter so wear items are usually a same-day pickup. On a push mower the biggest service items are the air filter and the spark plug — Lynn keeps those at the parts counter, same-day pickup if you walk in. The blade we'll sharpen on the bench while you wait, if it's not too gnarled.

For the in-town buyer with a flat lot and a tight Saturday schedule who isn't ready for a tractor and doesn't want a cordless, the Toro TurfMaster 30-inch Push Mower (22217) is the right size and platform. Talk to Damian in the service department (small engines, lawn, snow, Echo) — he'll tell you what holds up and what to expect from the platform. 705-253-7828 or stop in. Aaron's bench read: the push mowers that hold up are the ones that get the spark plug changed every couple seasons and the deck scraped clean of grass clippings. Two five-minute jobs that double the service life.

Name: 2026 Toro TimeCutter 42-inch Zero-Turn Mower (77404)
Short description: Toro TimeCutter 42-inch Zero-Turn Mower (77404) — The 42-inch deck handles the lawn in half the time for the half-acre-to-one-acre customer. Authorised Toro dealer service at Reyco.
Description:
If you've got a property where straight rows and tight pivots both matter, the Toro TimeCutter 42-inch Zero-Turn Mower (77404) is built for it. Zero-turns trade tractor steering for raw productivity — once you're comfortable on one, you'll cut the same lawn in half the time, with cleaner trim around obstacles.

Out across the cottage country lots, where most cottage and rural lots are obstacle courses (trees, septic mounds, gardens, gravel pads), the zero-turn pivot is what saves the time. The Toro TimeCutter 42-inch Zero-Turn Mower (77404) sits in the sweet spot for a half-acre to a full acre — anything smaller and the deck size is overkill; anything bigger and you'd want a commercial unit. The 42-inch deck pulls cleanly through long grass without bogging, which matters when the rain runs the grass three days past mowing day. Aaron points the customers who'd grown up on tractors toward this one when the lot makes the case for it — and most aren't sorry about the switch.

Reyco is an authorised Toro dealer — warranty work and platform-specific service come through our shop. Aaron runs the service side as Service Manager; Lynn manages the parts counter so wear items are usually a same-day pickup. That matters more on a zero-turn than on most platforms — the hydrostatic drive units, the spindle bearings, the deck mounts are all wear items that benefit from being looked at by a tech who knows the line. We see Toro zero-turns come back on schedule for routine deck-belt and spindle service around year four, which is normal. Less drama, more uptime.

For the rural property holder with the back forty to manage, the Toro TimeCutter 42-inch Zero-Turn Mower (77404) is the upgrade that changes the relationship to the lawn — Saturdays back, cleaner cut, less time on the seat. Stop by the showroom at 11 White Oak Drive East to see it on the floor, or call 705-253-7828 if you'd rather talk through whether it's the right fit.

Name: 2024 Cub Cadet ZT2-54 54-inch Zero-Turn Mower (17AIEAC3A10)
Short description: Cub Cadet ZT2-54 54-inch Zero-Turn Mower (17AIEAC3A10) — The 54-inch deck handles a Northern Ontario lawn in half the time. Authorised Cub Cadet dealer service from Aaron and Damian at White Oak Drive.
Description:
Aaron sees a lot of Cub Cadet ZT2 units come back for spring tune-ups every year — clean tune-ups, not warranty drama — and the Cub Cadet ZT2-54 54-inch Zero-Turn Mower (17AIEAC3A10) is one of the platforms that earns that read. For the retiree who finally bought the equipment he always wanted, this is the unit that pencils out as the upgrade from a residential tractor.

Out across the rural lots north of the city, where most cottage and rural lots are obstacle courses (trees, septic mounds, gardens, gravel pads), the zero-turn pivot is what saves the time. The Cub Cadet ZT2-54 54-inch Zero-Turn Mower (17AIEAC3A10) sits in the sweet spot for a half-acre to a full acre — anything smaller and the deck size is overkill; anything bigger and you'd want a commercial unit. The 54-inch deck pulls cleanly through long grass without bogging, which matters when the rain runs the grass three days past mowing day. Aaron points the customers who'd grown up on tractors toward this one when the lot makes the case for it — and most aren't sorry about the switch.

Reyco is an authorised Cub Cadet dealer — warranty work happens here in Sault Ste. Marie, no shipping the unit elsewhere. Aaron oversees the service side as Co-Owner and Service Manager; Damian handles the lawn-and-snow bench end to end; Lynn manages the parts counter. That matters more on a zero-turn than on most platforms — the hydrostatic drive units, the spindle bearings, the deck mounts are all wear items that benefit from being looked at by a tech who knows the line. We see Cub Cadet zero-turns come back on schedule for routine deck-belt and spindle service around year four, which is normal. Less drama, more uptime.

For the rural property holder with the back forty to manage, the Cub Cadet ZT2-54 54-inch Zero-Turn Mower (17AIEAC3A10) is the upgrade that changes the relationship to the lawn — Saturdays back, cleaner cut, less time on the seat. Aaron, our Service Manager, is at the showroom most days — drop in or call 705-253-7828 and he'll walk you through it.

Name: 2026 Cub Cadet XT1 ST54 54-inch Riding Mower FAB Enduro Series
Short description: Cub Cadet XT1 ST54 54-inch Riding Mower FAB Enduro Series — The 54-inch deck handles a real Northern Ontario lawn. Authorised Cub Cadet dealer service from Aaron and Damian at White Oak Drive.
Description:
If your lawn is enough work that a 42-inch deck would have you doing eight passes instead of five, the Cub Cadet XT1 ST54 54-inch Riding Mower FAB Enduro Series is the unit. Cub Cadet's XT1 platform was built around the in-town buyer with a flat lot and a tight Saturday schedule — the customer with real grass to cut, not just a strip in front of the house.

The XT1 platform is Cub Cadet's ride-on done right: hydrostatic drive (push the pedal, it goes), a comfortable seat for the long pass, and an electric PTO so engaging the deck is one button instead of a pull. For the customer mowing a half-acre Sault property up through a full lakefront acre, the geometry works — three or four passes per side and you're done. Up here, where the mowing window runs from late May to early October, you want a unit you can trust to start every Saturday — and that's where Cub Cadet's residential line earns its money. Aaron's been managing the service side here long enough to tell you which tier of the lineup actually pays off and which one's just hood badges — drop in and he'll walk you through it.

Reyco is an authorised Cub Cadet dealer — warranty work happens here in Sault Ste. Marie, no shipping the unit elsewhere. Aaron oversees the service side as Co-Owner and Service Manager; Damian handles the lawn-and-snow bench end to end; Lynn manages the parts counter. For the retiree who finally bought the equipment he always wanted, the trade-off pencils out: a unit that'll hold up across a decade of weekend mowing on the mixed cottage-and-rural lawns we get up here, with parts and service running through one shop instead of three. That's the part of the buy-here pitch that matters past year three — when the spindle starts to clunk or the deck belt walks, it's a half-hour conversation with Damian, not a six-week parts order.

Cub Cadet has been part of Reyco's lawn line since the early days, and we've serviced enough of these to know what holds up. Reyco's been on White Oak Drive East for more than 60 years. Stop by, talk to a tech, see the unit on the floor.

Name: 2026 Cub Cadet XT1 LT42 42-inch Riding Mower IntelliPOWER Enduro Series
Short description: Cub Cadet XT1 LT42 42-inch Riding Mower IntelliPOWER Enduro Series — The 42-inch deck handles a real Northern Ontario lawn. Authorised Cub Cadet dealer service from Aaron and Damian at White Oak Drive.
Description:
If your lawn is enough work that a 42-inch deck would have you doing eight passes instead of five, the Cub Cadet XT1 LT42 42-inch Riding Mower IntelliPOWER Enduro Series is the unit. Cub Cadet's XT1 platform was built around the property caretaker juggling four or five lakefront places — the customer with real grass to cut, not just a strip in front of the house.

The XT1 platform is Cub Cadet's ride-on done right: hydrostatic drive (push the pedal, it goes), a comfortable seat for the long pass, and an electric PTO so engaging the deck is one button instead of a pull. For the customer mowing a half-acre Sault property up through a full lakefront acre, the geometry works — three or four passes per side and you're done. Up here, where the mowing window runs from late May to early October, you want a unit you can trust to start every Saturday — and that's where Cub Cadet's residential line earns its money. Aaron's been managing the service side here long enough to tell you which tier of the lineup actually pays off and which one's just hood badges — drop in and he'll walk you through it.

Reyco is an authorised Cub Cadet dealer — warranty work happens here in Sault Ste. Marie, no shipping the unit elsewhere. Aaron oversees the service side as Co-Owner and Service Manager; Damian handles the lawn-and-snow bench end to end; Lynn manages the parts counter. For the in-town buyer with a flat lot and a tight Saturday schedule, the trade-off pencils out: a unit that'll hold up across a decade of weekend mowing on the rural lots north of the city, with parts and service running through one shop instead of three. That's the part of the buy-here pitch that matters past year three — when the spindle starts to clunk or the deck belt walks, it's a half-hour conversation with Damian, not a six-week parts order.

Cub Cadet has been part of Reyco's lawn line since the early days, and we've serviced enough of these to know what holds up. Drop in at 11 White Oak Drive East to see it on the floor. Lynn manages the parts counter and Ron can pull most wear items same-day.
